```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
pa <- sample(seq(0.25,0.75,0.05),1)
pba <- sample(seq(0.20,0.80,0.05),1)
ans <- pa*pba
vals <- round(c(ans,pba/pa,pa+pba,pa*(1-pba),pba),4)
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $P(A)=`r pa`$ e $P(B\mid A)=`r pba`$, qual é $P(A\cap B)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Pela regra da multiplicação,
$$P(A\cap B)=P(A)P(B\mid A)
=(`r pa`)(`r pba`)=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S4Q2_regra_multiplicacao
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
